kova-lpoa-v2-1-0.acceptedText:
Kova Services Inc.

Limited Power of Attorney

Authorization for Bill Negotiation Services

Version 2.1.0  |  Effective: April 6, 2026

ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED: This document must be reviewed by a licensed consumer protection attorney before any user is asked to sign it. This draft is prepared for legal review purposes.

IMPORTANT NOTICE TO PRINCIPAL

READ THIS DOCUMENT CAREFULLY BEFORE SIGNING. This is a legal document that gives Kova Services Inc. the power to act on your behalf in limited, specific ways. You have the right to revoke this authorization at any time. You are not required to sign this document to use Kova's other services.

1. PARTIES

Principal (You): The individual who creates a Kova account and executes this document.

Agent: Kova Services Inc., a Delaware corporation operating the Kova platform at getkova.com.

Effective Date: The date the Principal executes this document electronically.

2. GRANT OF LIMITED AUTHORITY

Subject to the restrictions in Section 3, the Principal hereby grants the Agent a LIMITED, NON-DURABLE, REVOCABLE power of attorney to perform ONLY the following acts on the Principal's behalf:

2.1 Authorized Actions

Contact service providers (as selected by the Principal) via telephone, live chat, or online portal to request rate reductions, loyalty discounts, promotional pricing, or billing credits

Identify as acting on behalf of the Principal and state the Principal's account information solely for the purpose of account verification and negotiation

Present competitor pricing information and loyalty tenure data to support negotiation

Request to speak with retention departments, supervisors, or customer loyalty teams

Accept or decline rate offers on the Principal's behalf, subject to the Principal's pre-approved minimum acceptable rate (if set)

Request written confirmation of any agreed-upon rate changes via email

2.2 Authorized Providers

This authorization applies ONLY to the specific service providers the Principal selects during the consent flow. The Principal may add or remove providers at any time through their account settings.

3. EXPRESS LIMITATIONS — WHAT KOVA CANNOT DO

The Agent is EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED from taking any of the following actions:

Canceling, downgrading, or upgrading any service plan or subscription

Making any purchase, adding any service, or incurring any charges on the Principal's behalf

Accessing, sharing, or transmitting the Principal's account passwords, PINs, or security credentials to any third party

Taking any action not directly related to rate negotiation

Acting on behalf of the Principal with any provider not expressly authorized

Entering into any contract, agreement, or commitment with a term exceeding 24 months without explicit separate written consent

Sharing the Principal's personal information with any party other than the authorized service providers

Acting under this authorization after it has been revoked

4. DURATION AND REVOCATION

4.1 Term

This authorization is effective upon execution and expires automatically on the earlier of: (a) twelve (12) months from the effective date, or (b) the date the Principal revokes this authorization.

4.2 Revocation

The Principal may revoke this authorization at any time, without cause, by any of the following methods:

Tapping "Revoke all authorizations" in the Kova app Settings screen

Sending an email to legal@getkova.com with the subject line "REVOKE AUTHORIZATION"

Replying "REVOKE" to any email received from Kova

Calling Kova support at the number listed on the Kova website

Revocation becomes effective within one (1) hour of receipt by Kova. Revocation does not affect obligations already incurred prior to revocation (including performance fees for negotiations already completed).

5. NON-DURABLE NATURE

THIS IS A NON-DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY. This authorization automatically terminates if the Principal becomes incapacitated or unable to make decisions. This document does not grant any authority beyond the specific actions described herein.

6. RECORDING AND DOCUMENTATION

The Principal acknowledges and consents that:

Telephone calls made under this authorization may be recorded for quality assurance and legal compliance purposes

A notice stating "This call may be recorded for quality assurance" will be announced at the start of every telephone call made under this authorization

Chat transcripts will be retained for a minimum of seven (7) years

All actions taken under this authorization are logged with timestamp, IP address, and device fingerprint in an immutable audit record

7. COMPENSATION

The Principal agrees to pay Kova a performance fee equal to fifteen percent (15%) of the annualized monthly savings achieved through a successful negotiation, as a one-time charge. This fee is calculated as: (old monthly rate - new monthly rate) x 12 x 0.15.

No fee is charged if a negotiation is unsuccessful. The fee is charged automatically to the Principal's payment method on file within 48 hours of a confirmed successful outcome.

8. PRINCIPAL'S REPRESENTATIONS

By executing this document, the Principal represents and warrants that:

The Principal is at least 18 years of age

The Principal is the account holder or authorized representative of the service accounts subject to negotiation

The Principal has full legal capacity to enter into this agreement

All information provided to Kova is accurate and complete

9. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION

The Principal acknowledges that: (a) this document is executed electronically pursuant to the Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (E-SIGN Act), 15 U.S.C. § 7001 et seq.; (b) the electronic signature is legally equivalent to a handwritten signature; (c) the Principal has had the opportunity to review the full document before signing; and (d) the Principal has scrolled through and reviewed the entire document.

10. GOVERNING LAW

This Limited Power of Attorney shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Arizona, without regard to conflict of law principles.

11. STATE-SPECIFIC PROVISIONS

California

California residents: This document complies with California Probate Code Section 4401 et seq. governing limited powers of attorney. The scope of this authorization is expressly limited to bill negotiation services as described herein and does not constitute a general power of attorney under California law.

Florida

Florida residents: This authorization complies with Florida Statutes Chapter 709. This is a limited, non-durable power of attorney. The agent may not delegate this authority to any sub-agent.

New York

New York residents: This authorization complies with the New York General Obligations Law Article 5, Title 15. The modifications and limitations stated herein are expressly incorporated.

12. SEVERABILITY

If any provision of this document is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

EXECUTION

BY ELECTRONICALLY SIGNING BELOW, THE PRINCIPAL CONFIRMS THAT: (1) the Principal has read and understood this entire document; (2) the Principal voluntarily consents to grant the limited authority described herein; (3) the Principal understands this is a legal document with binding effect; and (4) the Principal is at least 18 years of age.

_________________________________

_________________________________

Principal Signature (Electronic)

Date

Printed Full Legal Name: _________________________________________________________________

LPOA Version: 2.1.0  |  Document Hash: [SHA-256 hash generated at signing]

IP Address: [logged at signing]  |  Device ID: [logged at signing]

This document is executed in accordance with the E-SIGN Act. A PDF copy will be delivered to the Principal's email address on file immediately upon execution and stored in the Principal's encrypted Kova vault.

9. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION

kova-terms-of-service-v1-0-0.acceptedText:
Kova Services Inc.

Terms of Service

User Agreement for Kova Platform

Version 1.0.0  |  Effective: April 6, 2026

ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED: Draft document for legal review before public deployment.

1. ACCEPTANCE OF TERMS

By creating an account, accessing, or using the Kova platform (including the website at getkova.com and any mobile applications), you ("User") agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree, do not use the platform.

You must be at least 18 years of age to use Kova. By using the platform, you represent that you meet this requirement.

2. DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES

Kova Services Inc. provides three service modules:

2.1 Legal Shield

An AI-powered document analysis service that reviews contracts, leases, and agreements, identifies potentially unfavorable clauses, provides plain-English explanations, and generates suggested negotiation language. Legal Shield is NOT a law firm and does NOT provide legal advice. The analysis is informational only.

2.2 Money Saver

A bill negotiation service in which Kova, acting as authorized agent under a separately executed Limited Power of Attorney, contacts your service providers to negotiate lower rates on your behalf. The Money Saver service operates in phases as described in the pricing terms.

2.3 Build Smarter

A permit intelligence service for contractors that provides live municipal permit requirements, pre-filled application assistance, and regulatory change alerts by jurisdiction and trade type.

3. NOT LEGAL ADVICE

IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER: Kova is not a law firm. The Legal Shield service does not constitute legal advice and does not create an attorney-client relationship. Contract analysis provided by Kova is generated by artificial intelligence and is informational in nature only. You should consult a licensed attorney for advice specific to your legal situation, particularly before signing any contract based on Kova's analysis.

4. ACCOUNT REGISTRATION

You must provide accurate, complete, and current information during registration

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials

You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account

You must notify Kova immediately at legal@getkova.com of any unauthorized use of your account

Kova reserves the right to terminate accounts that violate these Terms

5. SUBSCRIPTION FEES AND BILLING

5.1 Legal Shield

Legal Shield is offered on a monthly or annual subscription basis at the rates displayed on the pricing page. Subscriptions automatically renew unless cancelled at least 24 hours before the renewal date.

5.2 Money Saver

Phase 1 (Script Generator): A flat fee of $9.99 per negotiation script generated, charged at the time of generation. Phase 2+ (Automated Negotiation): A performance fee of 15% of annualized monthly savings, charged as a one-time fee within 48 hours of confirmed successful negotiation. No fee is charged for unsuccessful negotiations.

5.3 Build Smarter

Build Smarter is offered on a monthly or annual per-firm subscription at the rates displayed on the pricing page, with additional per-seat fees for team members.

5.4 Payment Processing

All payments are processed by Stripe. Kova does not store complete payment card information. By providing payment information, you authorize Kova to charge the applicable fees to your payment method.

5.5 Refunds

Subscription fees are non-refundable except where required by applicable law. Performance fees for successful negotiations are non-refundable. If you believe a performance fee was charged in error, contact legal@getkova.com within 30 days.

6. PROHIBITED USES

You agree not to:

Use the platform for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any regulations

Provide false or misleading information about your service accounts or providers

Attempt to circumvent or manipulate Kova's fee calculation

Use the platform to harass, threaten, or intimidate service provider representatives

Attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or extract source code from the platform

Use automated tools to access the platform except through official APIs

Impersonate any person or entity, including Kova employees

Use the platform for any purpose other than personal, non-commercial bill negotiation and document review

7. DOCUMENT VAULT AND DATA SECURITY

Documents uploaded to Kova are encrypted at rest and in transit

Kova does not sell, license, or share your uploaded documents with third parties

Kova retains uploaded documents for the duration of your subscription plus 90 days after cancellation

You may request deletion of your documents at any time by contacting privacy@getkova.com

Kova uses commercially reasonable security measures but cannot guarantee absolute security

8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

The Kova platform, including all software, design, trademarks, and content, is owned by Kova Services Inc. and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the platform for its intended purpose.

You retain all rights to documents you upload. By uploading documents, you grant Kova a limited license to process and analyze those documents solely for the purpose of providing the contracted services.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, KOVA SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR: (a) any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages; (b) any loss of profits, revenue, data, or business opportunity; (c) any service disruptions or unauthorized access beyond Kova's reasonable control; or (d) any actions taken by service providers in response to negotiations.

KOVA'S TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING FROM THESE TERMS OR THE USE OF THE PLATFORM SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF: (a) the total fees paid by you to Kova in the 12 months preceding the claim, or (b) one hundred dollars ($100).

10. INDEMNIFICATION

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Kova Services Inc., its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from: (a) your use of the platform; (b) your violation of these Terms; or (c) your violation of any third-party rights.

11. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

11.1 Informal Resolution

Before initiating any formal dispute, you agree to contact Kova at legal@getkova.com and attempt to resolve the dispute informally for at least 30 days.

11.2 Binding Arbitration

ANY DISPUTE ARISING FROM OR RELATING TO THESE TERMS OR THE PLATFORM THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED INFORMALLY SHALL BE RESOLVED BY BINDING ARBITRATION UNDER THE RULES OF THE AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION. Arbitration shall take place in Arizona. The arbitrator's decision shall be final and binding.

11.3 Class Action Waiver

YOU WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO PARTICIPATE IN A CLASS ACTION LAWSUIT OR CLASS-WIDE ARBITRATION. All disputes must be brought in your individual capacity.

11.4 Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Arizona, without regard to conflict of law provisions.

12. MODIFICATIONS TO TERMS

Kova reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated via email at least 30 days before taking effect. Continued use of the platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the modified Terms.

13. TERMINATION

Kova may suspend or terminate your account for violation of these Terms, with or without notice. You may terminate your account at any time by canceling your subscription and contacting legal@getkova.com. Termination does not relieve you of outstanding payment obligations.

14. CONTACT

Kova Services Inc.

Kova Services Inc., [Address], Phoenix, AZ [ZIP]

legal@getkova.com

getkova.com

kova-privacy-policy-v1-0-0.acceptedText:
Kova Services Inc.

Privacy Policy

How Kova Collects, Uses, and Protects Your Data

Version 1.0.0  |  Effective: April 6, 2026

ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED: Draft document for legal review. Ensure CCPA and state-specific compliance before public deployment.

1. INTRODUCTION

Kova Services Inc. ("Kova," "we," "us," or "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use the Kova platform. Please read this policy carefully.

2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT

2.1 Information You Provide

Account information: name, email address, and password

Payment information: processed and stored by Stripe; Kova does not store complete card numbers

Documents: contracts, leases, NDAs, and other documents you upload to Legal Shield

Bill information: service provider accounts, billing amounts, and account details you provide for negotiation

Communications: emails, support messages, and feedback you send to Kova

Electronic signature: your typed name and device metadata collected at consent flow execution

2.2 Information Collected Automatically

Log data: IP address, browser type, device identifiers, and access timestamps

Usage data: features accessed, documents analyzed, and navigation patterns within the platform

Negotiation records: transcripts and outcomes of negotiations conducted on your behalf

Audit log: immutable record of all consent events including scroll depth, checkbox selections, and signature

2.3 Information from Third Parties

Plaid: bank account transaction data for bill monitoring and savings verification (only when you connect your bank account)

Service providers: confirmation and rate information received during negotiation sessions

3. HOW WE USE YOUR INFORMATION

We use the information we collect to:

Provide, operate, and improve the Kova platform and its features

Execute authorized negotiations with service providers on your behalf

Process payments and maintain billing records

Send transactional emails including welcome messages, analysis reports, authorization confirmations, and win notifications

Monitor bill changes through Plaid to calculate performance fees

Maintain the immutable audit log required for LPOA legal compliance

Detect and prevent fraud, unauthorized access, and abuse

Comply with legal obligations and respond to lawful requests

Aggregate and anonymize usage data for product improvement (no personally identifiable information is used)

We do not: sell your personal information, use your documents to train AI models, share your information with advertisers, or use your information for any purpose not listed above.

4. DOCUMENT PRIVACY

Your uploaded documents receive the highest level of protection:

All documents are encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption

Documents are encrypted in transit using TLS 1.3

Documents are stored in your private vault, accessible only to you

Kova employees do not access your documents except when required to resolve a technical support issue you have requested, and only with your consent

Documents are never used to train AI models or shared with any third party

Documents are retained for the duration of your subscription plus 90 days, then permanently deleted unless you request earlier deletion

5. SHARING OF INFORMATION

We share your information only in the following limited circumstances:

5.1 Service Providers

We use the following categories of service providers who process data on our behalf under data processing agreements:

Payment processing: Stripe

Cloud infrastructure: Amazon Web Services (encrypted storage)

Email delivery: Resend

Analytics: PostHog (anonymized usage data only)

Bank data: Plaid (only when you connect your bank account)

5.2 During Negotiations

When conducting negotiations on your behalf, we share only the minimum information necessary with your authorized service providers: your account number (for verification), and the negotiation script. We do not share your passwords, full financial information, or any other personal data.

5.3 Legal Requirements

We may disclose your information if required by law, court order, or governmental authority, or to protect the rights, property, or safety of Kova, our users, or the public.

5.4 Business Transfers

In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, user information may be transferred. You will be notified via email prior to any such transfer.

6. YOUR RIGHTS AND CHOICES

6.1 Access and Portability

You may request a copy of all personal data we hold about you by contacting privacy@getkova.com. We will respond within 30 days.

6.2 Correction

You may update your account information at any time through the account settings screen.

6.3 Deletion

You may request deletion of your account and all associated data by contacting privacy@getkova.com. We will complete deletion within 30 days, except for data we are required to retain by law (including LPOA audit logs, which are retained for 7 years for legal compliance).

6.4 Revocation of Negotiation Authorization

You may revoke authorization for negotiations at any time through the Settings screen or by emailing legal@getkova.com. Revocation takes effect within one hour.

6.5 California Residents (CCPA)

California residents have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act, including the right to know what personal information is collected, the right to opt out of the sale of personal information (note: Kova does not sell personal information), and the right to non-discrimination for exercising CCPA rights. To exercise your California rights, contact privacy@getkova.com.

7. DATA SECURITY

We implement commercially reasonable technical and organizational security measures including:

AES-256 encryption at rest for all user data and documents

TLS 1.3 encryption in transit

Row-level security on all database tables

Immutable audit logging with SHA-256 hash chain

Separate consent database with restricted access

Regular security reviews and penetration testing

Employee access controls and background screening

Despite these measures, no system is perfectly secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security and are not responsible for unauthorized access beyond our reasonable control. In the event of a data breach affecting your personal information, we will notify you within 72 hours as required by applicable law.

8. RETENTION

Data Category

Retention Period

Reason

Account data

Duration of subscription + 90 days

Service delivery

Tax/accounting law

Usage analytics

Product improvement

9. COOKIES AND TRACKING

Kova uses essential cookies necessary for platform operation (authentication, security). We use PostHog for anonymized analytics. We do not use advertising cookies or share data with ad networks.

10. CHILDREN'S PRIVACY

Kova is not directed at individuals under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from minors. If we become aware that a minor has created an account, we will delete their information immediately.

11. CHANGES TO THIS POLICY

We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Material changes will be communicated by email at least 30 days before taking effect. The date of the most recent revision is indicated at the top of this document.

12. CONTACT

Privacy inquiries: privacy@getkova.com

Legal inquiries: legal@getkova.com

paragraphs: Kova Services Inc.
Privacy Policy
How Kova Collects, Uses, and Protects Your Data
Version 1.0.0  |  Effective: April 6, 2026
ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED: Draft document for legal review. Ensure CCPA and state-specific compliance before public deployment.
1. INTRODUCTION

Kova Services Inc. ("Kova," "we," "us," or "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use the Kova platform. Please read this policy carefully.
2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT
2.1 Information You Provide
Account information: name, email address, and password
Payment information: processed and stored by Stripe; Kova does not store complete card numbers
Documents: contracts, leases, NDAs, and other documents you upload to Legal Shield
Bill information: service provider accounts, billing amounts, and account details you provide for negotiation
Communications: emails, support messages, and feedback you send to Kova
Electronic signature: your typed name and device metadata collected at consent flow execution
2.2 Information Collected Automatically
Log data: IP address, browser type, device identifiers, and access timestamps
Usage data: features accessed, documents analyzed, and navigation patterns within the platform
Negotiation records: transcripts and outcomes of negotiations conducted on your behalf
Audit log: immutable record of all consent events including scroll depth, checkbox selections, and signature
2.3 Information from Third Parties
Plaid: bank account transaction data for bill monitoring and savings verification (only when you connect your bank account)
Service providers: confirmation and rate information received during negotiation sessions
3. HOW WE USE YOUR INFORMATION
We use the information we collect to:
Provide, operate, and improve the Kova platform and its features
Execute authorized negotiations with service providers on your behalf
Process payments and maintain billing records
Send transactional emails including welcome messages, analysis reports, authorization confirmations, and win notifications
Monitor bill changes through Plaid to calculate performance fees
Maintain the immutable audit log required for LPOA legal compliance
Detect and prevent fraud, unauthorized access, and abuse
Comply with legal obligations and respond to lawful requests
Aggregate and anonymize usage data for product improvement (no personally identifiable information is used)
We do not: sell your personal information, use your documents to train AI models, share your information with advertisers, or use your information for any purpose not listed above.
4. DOCUMENT PRIVACY
Your uploaded documents receive the highest level of protection:
All documents are encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption
Documents are encrypted in transit using TLS 1.3
Documents are stored in your private vault, accessible only to you
Kova employees do not access your documents except when required to resolve a technical support issue you have requested, and only with your consent
Documents are never used to train AI models or shared with any third party
Documents are retained for the duration of your subscription plus 90 days, then permanently deleted unless you request earlier deletion
5. SHARING OF INFORMATION
We share your information only in the following limited circumstances:
5.1 Service Providers
We use the following categories of service providers who process data on our behalf under data processing agreements:
Payment processing: Stripe
Cloud infrastructure: Amazon Web Services (encrypted storage)
Email delivery: Resend
Analytics: PostHog (anonymized usage data only)
Bank data: Plaid (only when you connect your bank account)
5.2 During Negotiations

When conducting negotiations on your behalf, we share only the minimum information necessary with your authorized service providers: your account number (for verification), and the negotiation script. We do not share your passwords, full financial information, or any other personal data.
5.3 Legal Requirements
We may disclose your information if required by law, court order, or governmental authority, or to protect the rights, property, or safety of Kova, our users, or the public.
5.4 Business Transfers
In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, user information may be transferred. You will be notified via email prior to any such transfer.
6. YOUR RIGHTS AND CHOICES
6.1 Access and Portability
You may request a copy of all personal data we hold about you by contacting privacy@getkova.com. We will respond within 30 days.
6.2 Correction
You may update your account information at any time through the account settings screen.
6.3 Deletion

You may request deletion of your account and all associated data by contacting privacy@getkova.com. We will complete deletion within 30 days, except for data we are required to retain by law (including LPOA audit logs, which are retained for 7 years for legal compliance).
6.4 Revocation of Negotiation Authorization
You may revoke authorization for negotiations at any time through the Settings screen or by emailing legal@getkova.com. Revocation takes effect within one hour.
6.5 California Residents (CCPA)

California residents have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act, including the right to know what personal information is collected, the right to opt out of the sale of personal information (note: Kova does not sell personal information), and the right to non-discrimination for exercising CCPA rights. To exercise your California rights, contact privacy@getkova.com.
7. DATA SECURITY
We implement commercially reasonable technical and organizational security measures including:
AES-256 encryption at rest for all user data and documents
TLS 1.3 encryption in transit
Row-level security on all database tables
Immutable audit logging with SHA-256 hash chain
Separate consent database with restricted access
Regular security reviews and penetration testing
Employee access controls and background screening

Despite these measures, no system is perfectly secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security and are not responsible for unauthorized access beyond our reasonable control. In the event of a data breach affecting your personal information, we will notify you within 72 hours as required by applicable law.
8. RETENTION

9. COOKIES AND TRACKING
Kova uses essential cookies necessary for platform operation (authentication, security). We use PostHog for anonymized analytics. We do not use advertising cookies or share data with ad networks.
10. CHILDREN'S PRIVACY

Kova is not directed at individuals under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from minors. If we become aware that a minor has created an account, we will delete their information immediately.
11. CHANGES TO THIS POLICY

We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Material changes will be communicated by email at least 30 days before taking effect. The date of the most recent revision is indicated at the top of this document.

kova-beta-nda-v1-0-0.acceptedText:
Kova Services Inc.

Beta User Non-Disclosure Agreement

Mutual Confidentiality Agreement for Beta Testers

Version 1.0.0  |  Effective: April 6, 2026

This is a 1-page working NDA for beta users. Simple, enforceable, and clear. Have beta users sign before showing them the product.

PARTIES

Disclosing Party: Kova Services Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Kova")

Receiving Party: ________________________________________ ("Beta User")

Effective Date: ________________________________________

1. PURPOSE

The parties wish to explore a potential business relationship in which the Beta User will test and evaluate Kova's pre-release software platform (the "Beta Product"). In connection with this evaluation, Kova will disclose certain confidential and proprietary information to the Beta User.

2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

"Confidential Information" means any and all non-public information disclosed by Kova to the Beta User, including but not limited to:

The Beta Product software, features, functionality, and user interface

Product roadmap, pricing strategy, and business model

Technical architecture, database design, and AI system details

User research, market research, and competitive analysis

Any information marked as "confidential" or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the context

3. OBLIGATIONS OF BETA USER

The Beta User agrees to:

Keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential

Not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent from Kova

Use the Confidential Information solely to evaluate the Beta Product

Not reverse-engineer, decompile, or copy any part of the Beta Product

Not take screenshots, recordings, or other captures of the Beta Product for distribution

Promptly notify Kova if the Beta User becomes aware of any unauthorized disclosure

4. EXCEPTIONS

The confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Beta User; (b) was rightfully known to the Beta User before disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the Beta User without use of Confidential Information; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law or court order (provided Beta User gives Kova prompt written notice).

5. FEEDBACK

Any feedback, suggestions, or ideas the Beta User provides regarding the Beta Product ("Feedback") shall be owned exclusively by Kova. The Beta User hereby assigns all right, title, and interest in any Feedback to Kova. Kova may use Feedback without restriction or compensation.

6. TERM

This Agreement is effective as of the Effective Date and continues for two (2) years. The confidentiality obligations survive termination of this Agreement.

7. KOVA'S RECIPROCAL OBLIGATION

Kova agrees to keep confidential any personal or business information provided by the Beta User during beta testing, including any documents uploaded for testing purposes, and to use such information solely for product development purposes.

8. NO LICENSE

Nothing in this Agreement grants the Beta User any license, right, or interest in the Beta Product beyond the right to evaluate it for the stated purpose.

9. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Arizona. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Arizona.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality of the Beta Product and supersedes all prior discussions on this subject.

SIGNATURES

KOVA SERVICES INC.

Authorized Signatory

Name and Title

Date

BETA USER

Signature

Printed Name

Date

Free NDA template alternative: docracy.com — search "mutual NDA" for a simple 1-page version if you need something you can sign today before attorney review is complete.
